comments.text:
“Unlike many other regions in Europe, the Welch language is still very much alive and actively spoken here.”

Is there an expectation that Welch should be spoken in other regions in Europe?  What an absolutely stupid thing to say

Unlike in Wales, most citizens of Germany speak German.  That is what you sound like. 

Maybe:  “In other areas of the world, ancestral languages have succumbed to global languages, but not in Wales where its ancient Celtic language is still widely spoken.

comments.author: @Vivid_Planet_TV
comments.text: Watching this made me realize that some places still feel connected to their history in a way that's hard to find today. Wales isn't just beautiful scenery and castles—there's a sense of identity and heritage that seems woven into the landscape itself. Thank you for sharing such a thoughtful journey.
